sections.paragraph:
συναρμολογέω (ἁρμός, λέγω; only in Christian writers) to join together so as to form a coherent entity, fit/join together pass. of a building Eph 2:21. Of the body (cp. συμβιβάζω 1a) 4:16.—M-M. TW.

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 966
